signals.thesis_setup: Long $95 call entered 4/10 at $7.70/contract; CoreWeave was around $92 at signal (ATM, near-the-money). Entry was a continuation read after the prior-day fire: same flow profile repeated, with two more golden-block-trade prints over $1M premium and the put/call dollar ratio shifting further in favor of calls. Watchlist designation reflected position-sizing constraint, not lower conviction. Spot now $125, position is +309% (peak +356%). The ATM structure has converted to deep ITM with delta near 1.0 and gamma low; mostly a delta-convexity ride at this point.
signals.thesis_catalyst: CoreWeave is the AI infrastructure pure-play: GPU cloud rental capacity dedicated to LLM training and inference, with Microsoft and Meta as anchor customers. The structural story is irreplaceable scarcity (Nvidia GPU allocation flows here first), but volatility is in customer concentration and contract renewal timing. Recent catalyst was the Q1 announcement of expanded NVDA Blackwell allocation, which extends the supply moat through 2027. AMD print Tuesday AH adds tertiary readthrough: an AMD beat broadens the GPU compute narrative beyond Nvidia, marginally pressuring CRWV's scarcity thesis but boosting overall sector tape.
signals.thesis_macro: Tightening conditions are a real headwind to a 60x sales multiple, but CRWV has been the exception because it is treated as the cleanest expression of AI capex demand. Risk is that if NDX rolls over hard, CRWV gets sold first as the speculative apex; offset is that institutional positioning has been one-sided long since Q1, so a flush would be quick rather than grinding.
signals.thesis_risk: Stop level $3.85 (50% of entry premium). DTE 4 days, theta now dominant - even a flat tape produces ~5% daily decay from here. Invalidates: spot $115 break (gives back the post-Blackwell breakout), or NDX -2% session that drags AI long-tail names. Mathematical case is to trim a portion into the AMD print Tue AH; even a beat sees CRWV give back IV crush. Position resolves at -50% or expiry.

signals.thesis_setup: Long $375 call entered 4/15 at $6.50/contract; Microsoft was $387 at signal (ITM, 3.1% in-the-money). Entry caught a multi-day institutional accumulation pattern around Azure capex commentary tied to FQ3 earnings: block trades over $1M premium hit the $370-$390 May chain across three sessions, plus the put/call dollar split flipped from 1.4 puts/calls to 1.9 calls/puts in the 48 hours pre-entry. Dark pool aggregate showed MSFT in the top-5 by dollar volume for the week. Spot is now $410, position is +499% (peak +841%). Deep ITM structure converts cleanly into delta as the rally extends.
signals.thesis_catalyst: Microsoft sits at the center of every AI capex narrative: Azure as the enterprise AI platform, GitHub Copilot at scale, and the OpenAI partnership that turns OpenAI's product velocity into Azure compute revenue. Active catalyst slate is dense: AMD print Tuesday AH (datacenter capex readthrough), broader hyperscaler capex commentary mid-month, and Build 2026 in late May. Earnings are behind us (4/29 print drove the initial breakout), so this position is purely a delta-convexity ride on post-print follow-through, not an earnings binary. Watch for any FY26 capex revision that would re-rate the multiple either way.
signals.thesis_macro: Tightening tape (10Y at 4.45%, dollar firm, gold weak) is a headwind for growth multiples broadly, but Microsoft is the rare name benefiting from a 'flight to quality within tech': institutions selling speculative AI long-tail names rotate into MSFT/GOOGL as durable cash-flow proxies. The AAPL stop today is the tell that not all mega-cap is treated equally; MSFT differentiates because the AI revenue is already in the run-rate, not promised in a future product cycle.
signals.thesis_risk: Stop level $3.25 (50% of entry premium). Position is deep ITM so theta is modest relative to extrinsic-heavy OTM peers; the bigger risk is a sharp NDX drawdown rolling MSFT back toward $390 and compressing the intrinsic. DTE 11 days carries through the AMD print (Tue AH) + ISM Services (Tue AM) + NFP (Fri). Invalidates: spot $395 break with no fast recovery, or any AI capex commentary suggesting deceleration. Position resolves at -50% or expiry. No discretionary trim window before stop trigger.

signals.thesis_setup: Long $380 call entered 4/16 at $12.80/contract; TSM was around $375 at signal (ATM, 1.3% ITM). Entry on a slow-build foundry-monopoly thesis: TSM is the structural beneficiary of every advanced-node demand cycle (Nvidia, AMD, Apple, Qualcomm all source from TSM). Flow read was less burst-shaped than the MU/CRWV trades, more institutional accumulation pattern. Spot now $400+ area, position is +133% (peak +221%). The 5/22 DTE provides 18 days of cushion through AMD print + NFP + the next FOMC mid-month.
signals.thesis_catalyst: TSM is the foundry monopoly on advanced nodes (3nm, 2nm, A16 future). Every AI accelerator on the planet ships from TSM, capacity allocated 18-24 months in advance. The structural story is monolithic; the catalysts are quarter-to-quarter capex commentary determining pace of capacity expansion. Earnings 7/16 (well outside DTE). Active catalysts in DTE: any hyperscaler GPU allocation commentary, Apple A19 chip volume guidance, and the Taiwan-strait political backdrop that re-prices risk premium periodically.
signals.thesis_macro: Tightening tape is a modest headwind to a name with a 22x forward multiple, much less crowded than US-listed AI names. Taiwan-strait risk is the structural overhang that periodically gets re-priced; if the political tape stays quiet, multiple expansion has room to run. AMD print Tue AH is tertiary readthrough.
signals.thesis_risk: Stop level $6.40 (50% of entry premium). DTE 18 days, theta moderate. Invalidates: spot $385 break (gives back the breakout), or any Taiwan-strait headline-driven flush. The longer DTE absorbs IV crush from AMD print well. Position resolves at -50% or expiry.

signals.thesis_setup: Long $350 call entered 4/20 at $10.00/contract; Alphabet was $356 at signal (ATM, 1.7% ITM). Entry on a fresh institutional rotation pattern: GOOGL had lagged the AI capex names through April, but the flow signature shifted on the morning of 4/20 with three institutional buy-blocks over $750K premium on the May call side, plus repeat-rule fires on the $345-$355 strikes. Dark pool aggregate showed GOOGL net buyers across executed-at-bid blocks. Spot is now $389, position is +244% (peak +276%). The catch-up trade played as anticipated: rotation from MSFT/AMZN crowding into the lagging mega-cap.
signals.thesis_catalyst: Alphabet has three independent AI revenue streams: Search ad revenue (which Gemini-powered AI Overviews monetize incrementally), Cloud (closing the gap on AWS/Azure with TPU-differentiated cost economics), and YouTube optionality (re-rated if/when YouTube TV becomes a live-streaming foothold). Active catalyst right now is data-center-build acceleration and the Gemini 3.0 inference capacity rollout, both expected May-June. Earnings are past (4/24); position is a follow-through delta ride, not an earnings setup.
signals.thesis_macro: GOOGL benefits from the same AI-capex-only-bid dynamic as MSFT, with one additional tailwind: it is the cheapest mega-cap AI name on a forward earnings basis (~22x vs MSFT 35x), making it the institutional rotation target when more crowded names extend. Tightening tape pressure is offset by the multiple cushion.
signals.thesis_risk: Stop level $5.00 (50% of entry premium). DTE 11 days, theta moderate at this delta. Invalidates: spot $375 break (gives back the post-rotation breakout), or any negative AI Overview engagement data point that puts search monetization at risk. Position resolves at -50% or expiry.

signals.thesis_setup: Long $210 call entered 4/20 (peak premium ~$13, current ~$5); NVDA was around $200 at signal (OTM, 5% from spot). Entry on the late-cycle AI-leadership extension thesis. Position peaked at +249% in mid-April when NVDA spiked to $215+, then rolled over hard with the broader tech tape; current return -65%, deeply underwater and approaching the published rule. Net mathematical case: the position is ~15% premium away from triggering closeout.
signals.thesis_catalyst: Nvidia is the AI accelerator near-monopoly, with Blackwell ramp + Rubin roadmap providing multi-quarter visibility. The structural story is intact, but the position is suffering from a timing problem: peak entered ahead of the late-April tech rotation, and tightening macro has pressured the multiple. Next earnings 5/28 (after DTE expiry), so this position cannot benefit from a fundamental catalyst inside the contract. Position resolves on tape direction, currently against.
signals.thesis_macro: Tightening tape is the active headwind: NVDA carries the highest growth premium of the mega-cap AI names, so multiple compression hits hardest here. The cohort selling that took out AAPL today is the same dynamic pressuring NVDA. AMD print Tue AH is a tertiary risk: an AMD beat could trigger AI-cohort relief, but the position is too underwater for a small bounce to rescue.
signals.thesis_risk: Stop level approximately $6.50 (50% of entry premium), currently spot is around $185 area and option ~$5. Position is mathematically at-or-near closeout. Invalidates: spot $180 break or NDX -1% session that takes NVDA sub-$185. The thesis was correct directionally for the first leg (peaked +249%) but the tape rolled before exit discipline took the win. Position resolves at -50% or expiry.

signals.thesis_setup: Long GLD $470 call entered 4/20; gold was around $4500 area at signal (OTM strike). Entry on a continued safe-haven gold thesis as DXY had been bid into the new month. Position is currently -95% (peak +1%); deeply at the stop. Gold has fallen from $4800 to $4524 over the past week as yields broke higher and the dollar firmed: the textbook tightening signal that pressures gold.
signals.thesis_catalyst: Gold's near-term drivers are real yields (10Y TIPS) and dollar direction. Catalyst slate: Friday NFP determines whether tightening trade extends (pressures gold further) or reverses (relief rally). Fed speakers Tue (Bowman + Barr) could provide commentary that shifts real-yield expectations. The structural gold-as-debasement narrative is intact long-term but the cyclical setup is against the position right now.
signals.thesis_macro: Tightening conditions (yields up, dollar bid) are the direct headwind to gold longs. Position fights the macro read; idiosyncratic catalyst would need to overcome the rate pressure. The recent flow tells (continued put buying on GLD) confirm institutional positioning is bearish near-term.
signals.thesis_risk: Stop level approximately $2.25 (50% of entry premium), currently option around $0.20 area. Position is at the published rule. Invalidates: confirmed - gold has broken below the entry support and yields broke above the resistance level that anchored the thesis. Position resolves at -50% or expiry.

signals.thesis_setup: Long SLV $75 call entered 4/20; SLV was around $74 area at signal (ATM, near the money). Entry on a silver-as-industrial-metal thesis (electrification + AI datacenter copper/silver demand). Position is currently -92% (peak +5%); also at the stop. Silver has dropped from $77 to $73.17 over the past week, dragged by the same tightening-tape dynamic that hit gold but with extra leverage from industrial-demand uncertainty.
signals.thesis_catalyst: Silver has dual character: monetary metal (correlates with gold) and industrial metal (electronics, solar, EV electrification). The AI datacenter copper/silver demand thesis is real but slow-moving. Near-term drivers are identical to gold (real yields, dollar). No specific catalyst inside DTE that shifts the setup, this position resolves on macro tape direction.
signals.thesis_macro: Same as gold: tightening pressure is the active headwind. Silver typically has higher beta than gold to the same macro factors, which has played out: -3.7% on 5/4 vs gold -2.3%. Position fights the macro read and is now at the published rule.
signals.thesis_risk: Stop level approximately $0.75 (50% of entry premium), currently option pennies. Position is at the published rule. Invalidates: confirmed, silver has broken below entry support. Position resolves at -50% or expiry.

signals.thesis_setup: Long IWM $265 PUT entered 4/20 at $3.15/contract; IWM was around $275 at signal (OTM put, 3.6% from spot). Entry on a Russell-2000 weakness thesis driven by tightening macro: small caps are the most yield-sensitive equity factor, and yields breaking 4.40% was the trigger. Position peaked at +40% as IWM dropped through $268 in late April; current return -62% as IWM has rallied back to $278 area. The position is the lone bearish expression in the book and is now 38% above the published rule.
signals.thesis_catalyst: IWM tracks the Russell 2000, which is dominated by small-cap regional banks, real estate, and biotech: all yield-sensitive sectors. Active macro catalyst is Friday NFP: a strong print extends the tightening trade and the put thesis re-engages; a weak print triggers a dovish reset that further hurts the put. Friday is the binary moment.
signals.thesis_macro: The put is aligned with the tightening tape on direction, but the actual price action of the past two weeks (small caps rallying on hedge unwinds) has been against the position. The flow tells from today (large IWM put unwinds) suggest hedges are coming off, which is bullish for the underlying and bearish for the position.
signals.thesis_risk: Stop level $1.58 (50% of entry premium). DTE 11 days, theta is significant on an OTM put. Invalidates: IWM $282 break (gives back the entire range), or NFP Friday surprise to the upside. The position is mathematically near the stop and will likely close at the published rule unless small caps take a sharp reversal lower. Position resolves at -50% or expiry.

signals.thesis_setup: Long VG $12.5 call entered 4/24 at $1.55/contract; Vistagen was around $11.50 at signal (OTM, 8% from spot). Entry on FDA milestone catalyst: Vistagen's intranasal anxiolytic (PH94B/Itruvone) had a PDUFA target date approaching, and option flow had picked up on the May $12.50 strike. Position is +17% (peak +19%); slow-build position waiting for catalyst resolution.
signals.thesis_catalyst: Vistagen's lead asset is Itruvone (PH94B), a fast-onset intranasal anxiolytic with a novel mechanism (pheromone receptor pathway). The PDUFA decision is the binary catalyst inside DTE: approval triggers a likely 2-3x move higher; rejection or CRL triggers a 60%+ drawdown. No earnings inside DTE; this is purely an FDA-driven event trade.
signals.thesis_macro: Small-cap biotech is decoupled from broader macro tape on event windows; the position resolves on the FDA decision regardless of NDX direction or yields. Tightening tape is a marginal headwind because biotech multiples are duration-sensitive, but the binary catalyst dominates.
signals.thesis_risk: Stop level $0.78 (50% of entry premium). DTE 11 days. Binary catalyst inside DTE means the position will see significant move on PDUFA decision; sizing was light because the asymmetric outcome is well-understood. Invalidates: any pre-PDUFA setback (manufacturing inspection, late-cycle review delay), or PDUFA decision unfavorable. Position resolves at -50% or expiry.

signals.thesis_setup: Long $510 call entered 4/28 at $26.77/contract; Micron was $522 at signal (ITM, 2.4%). Entry on a tape-reader flag: 18 institutional block trades over $500K premium hit the May call chain in the first two hours of trade, with 12 of them golden-block-prints over $1M premium. Pattern was identical to the pre-Q3 NVDA print where HBM tightness commentary was the rumor. Spot rallied through $560 then $578 over the week; position is +160% (peak +206%). Greeks at 5/4 close: delta 0.91, theta -$1.31/day (~1.84% daily decay), IV 96.78%. The math signals a partial trim ahead of AMD print and IV crush.
signals.thesis_catalyst: Micron is the HBM (High-Bandwidth Memory) leader for AI accelerators; every Nvidia GPU and AMD MI-series accelerator ships with Micron, Samsung, or SK Hynix HBM, and Micron is winning the qualification battle on next-gen HBM4. AMD print Tue AH is the proximate catalyst: a beat with strong datacenter commentary cascades into MU readthrough Wed open; a miss compresses the cohort multiple. Position was sized on flow conviction; the trim case at +160% is mathematical (theta + IV crush eats positive deltas regardless of direction).
signals.thesis_macro: Tightening tape is a headwind to growth multiples but semi memory has been the AI proxy of choice for institutional AI exposure that refuses to chase the apex names. MU benefits from being a 'second-derivative' AI play: as long as the GPU buildout continues, HBM ships. Cyclical risk: memory has historically been a commodity, and any HBM oversupply commentary would compress the multiple sharply.
signals.thesis_risk: Stop level $13.39 (50% of entry premium). DTE 4 days, theta now dominant. Invalidates: AMD miss with bearish HBM commentary, or spot $560 break that gives back the breakout. The mathematical trim case at +160% is real: even an AMD beat sees ~14% IV crush which eats half of any positive directional move. Trim a portion ahead of print, ride residual through Friday. Position resolves at -50% or expiry.

signals.thesis_setup: Long $500 call entered 4/28 at $31.60/contract same-day as the $510C watchlist; Micron was $522 at signal (ITM, 4.4%). The deeper-ITM strike was the higher-conviction expression, with the $510C watchlist as a secondary. Both fired on the same HBM tightness flow read. Spot now $578; position is +148% (peak +190%). The deeper-ITM structure has lower theta drag than the $510C but also lower upside leverage on continued rally.
signals.thesis_catalyst: Identical underlying thesis to the $510C: HBM leadership + AMD print Tue AH readthrough + cohort dynamics. The $500C deeper-ITM strike is the higher-conviction expression because deeper ITM means more delta capture and less IV-crush sensitivity in absolute terms.
signals.thesis_macro: Same as $510C: AI-capex bid overriding tightening headwind, with HBM as the second-derivative play.
signals.thesis_risk: Stop level $15.80 (50% of entry premium). DTE 4 days. Invalidates: same as $510C. The $500C deeper-ITM structure is less theta-exposed but the AMD print binary still applies. Position resolves at -50% or expiry.

signals.thesis_setup: Long $165 call entered 4/28 at $13.50/contract; Oracle was $172 at signal (ITM, 4.2%). Entry on an unusual flow read: ORCL had been quiet for weeks, but a single golden block trade over $2M premium on the May $165 strike fired pre-market 4/28, followed by a put/call ratio inversion through the morning. Thesis was AI-cloud-narrative continuation: the OpenAI infrastructure partnership expansion was rumored to include additional capacity allocations. Spot dipped underwater early (low watermark -16% intra-position), then flipped green as the rumor was confirmed. Now +30% (peak +48%), position is back at thesis-on.
signals.thesis_catalyst: Oracle's pivot from on-prem database into Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) is the secular story; the OpenAI partnership is the accelerant. OpenAI is reportedly committing multiple-billion-dollar capacity at OCI through 2027, materially changing Oracle's cloud growth trajectory. Active catalyst is any expansion of that partnership or new hyperscaler-tier customer wins. Earnings 6/19 (well outside DTE), so this resolves on flow + sentiment, not a fundamental binary.
signals.thesis_macro: Tightening conditions are mixed for Oracle: the legacy database business is a duration-cash-flow positive, but OCI valuation is multiple-sensitive. Net read: position aligns with the AI-capex bid that is overriding macro headwind in the durable mega-cap names.
signals.thesis_risk: Stop level $6.75 (50% of entry premium). DTE 11 days. Invalidates: spot $165 break (gives back the OCI re-rating), or any commentary suggesting OpenAI-Oracle capacity is overstated. The early underwater drawdown happened on macro flush; the recovery suggests the thesis is intact. Position resolves at -50% or expiry.

signals.thesis_setup: Long QQQ $649 call entered 4/28 at $17.94/contract; QQQ was around $645 at signal (ATM, 0.6% ITM). Index proxy entry on a continued NDX strength thesis: post-earnings season had reinforced the AI capex narrative across MSFT/GOOGL/AMZN, and QQQ flow that morning showed cleanup buying on the index calls. Spot now $660+, position is +41% (peak +60%). Index proxy is the cleanest expression of NDX direction.
signals.thesis_catalyst: QQQ is the NDX index ETF (top-100 Nasdaq market-cap weighted, AAPL/MSFT/NVDA/GOOGL/AMZN are 40%+ of weight). Active catalyst is AMD print Tue AH (which feeds NDX via semis weight) plus Friday NFP (which determines macro tape direction). Earnings season is winding down, so individual-name binaries are diluted; tape direction is now the dominant driver.
signals.thesis_macro: Index proxy means the position carries the macro tape directly. Tightening conditions are a headwind, but the AI-capex bid in mega-cap names provides offset. Position will move with NDX direction; AMD print Tue AH is the proximate catalyst, NFP Fri is the macro binary.
signals.thesis_risk: Stop level $8.97 (50% of entry premium). DTE 4 days, theta is now meaningful. Invalidates: NDX -1% session that takes QQQ sub-$650, or AMD miss + cohort drawdown. The 5/8 expiry concentrates the AMD + NFP binary into a tight window. Trim partial ahead of AMD if position runs further. Position resolves at -50% or expiry.

Entry was a continuation/momentum size-up after the 4/20 watchlist position, executed when the post-earnings move proved durable into 4/30. Flow that day showed continued institutional accumulation: two blocks over $1.5M premium on the closer-dated chain plus dark pool aggregate persisting in the buyer-led split. Spot now $389, position is +82% (peak +100%). The 5/8 expiry is the shorter-DTE expression vs the 5/15 primary.
signals.thesis_catalyst: Same business thesis as the 5/15 primary: three-stream AI revenue with the cheapest mega-cap multiple. The 5/8 expiry adds short-dated theta pressure but captures the immediate AMD print Tue AH + Friday NFP into a tighter catalyst window. If AMD beats, the AI cohort tape lifts and GOOGL participates; if AMD misses, the watchlist designation and shorter DTE means this position takes a sharper hit than the 5/15 primary.
signals.thesis_macro: Same as the 5/15 primary: AI-capex bid + multiple cushion + tightening offset. The shorter DTE makes it more sensitive to NFP Friday's macro-binary outcome.
signals.thesis_risk: Stop level $9.25 (50% of entry premium). DTE 4 days. Invalidates: spot $385 break (gives back the post-print breakout), or AMD miss + cohort drawdown. The 5/8 expiry vs the 5/15 primary means trim discipline is sharper - the close of session Friday is the binary moment. Position resolves at -50% or expiry.

signals.thesis_setup: Long $260 call entered 4/30 at $5.88/contract; Amazon was $245 at signal (OTM, 6.1% from spot). Entry triggered on AWS earnings beat overnight: AWS year-on-year growth re-accelerated to 19%, the first acceleration in six quarters. Flow that morning showed multiple institutional buy-blocks over $400K premium across the $250-$265 strike chain, plus an unusual-volume read of 4.2x on May options. Spot rallied to $260+ within three sessions; position is +138% (peak +197%). Per-ticker dark pool drill on the morning of 5/4 showed institutional distribution patterns starting to surface, which informed the partial-trim recommendation in the midday post.
signals.thesis_catalyst: Amazon's AI capex story is the AWS Trainium/Inferentia silicon investment plus the Anthropic equity stake (~$8B). Active catalyst is AWS re:Invent narrative continuing through the spring, plus Q2 capex guidance that will be revisited in mid-quarter color. Retail business is largely a hedge: margin pressure exists, but Prime engagement is durable. Position is a follow-through delta ride on the post-earnings AWS reacceleration; institutional buyers are taking profits as spot extends, which is the trim-partial signal.
signals.thesis_macro: Tightening tape favors durable cash-flow mega-caps; AMZN qualifies along with MSFT/GOOGL. Risk is that retail-side margin commentary in any future Q2 update gets weighted more heavily in a yields-up environment, where consumer discretionary multiples compress. AWS reacceleration is the offset.
signals.thesis_risk: Stop level $2.94 (50% of entry premium). DTE 11 days. Invalidates: spot $250 break (gives back the post-print follow-through), or any AWS growth reversal commentary. Per-ticker DP shows distribution; partial trim already executed, residual position rides through AMD print Tue AH and into Friday NFP. Position resolves at -50% or expiry.

The 5/15 DTE adds the post-AMD recovery window: if AMD beats and the cohort drives higher into Wed-Thu, this position captures more of the move; if AMD misses and the cohort compresses, the extra week reduces the trim-discipline pace relative to the 5/8 expiries.

The thesis is identical: NDX direction following AMD print + NFP.

signals.thesis_setup: Long $95 call entered 5/1 at $11.10/contract; AXT was around $98 at signal (ATM, 3% ITM). Entry on a small-cap compound-semi flow read: AXT is a $1.5B market cap name that supplies indium phosphide (InP) and gallium arsenide (GaAs) wafers used in optical transceivers for AI datacenter networking. The flow signature was unusual for a name this size: two institutional block trades over $200K premium and a put/call ratio inversion. Spot now $115 area, position is +57% (peak +62%). Thesis is playing as anticipated: AI datacenter optical transceiver demand accelerating ahead of supplier scale.
signals.thesis_catalyst: AXT supplies the substrate wafers for high-speed optical transceivers (Coherent, Lumentum, Marvell are downstream customers) which connect AI accelerators inside hyperscaler datacenters. The structural story is that 800G and 1.6T optical-link adoption is accelerating, and InP supply is constrained. Earnings 5/8 (next week, inside DTE) is a binary catalyst with high IV crush risk on print regardless of direction. Active catalyst beyond earnings is any hyperscaler datacenter optical-buildout commentary.
signals.thesis_macro: Small-cap status makes AXT vulnerable to IWM/Russell-2000 selling pressure: when small caps get rotated out broadly, AXT goes with them regardless of fundamental fit. The compound-semi specialty does provide some idiosyncratic protection. Tightening tape is a headwind for small-cap risk in general.
signals.thesis_risk: Stop level $5.55 (50% of entry premium). DTE 11 days. Earnings 5/8 inside DTE: IV is currently elevated, post-print IV crush will eat ~15-20% of option premium even on a flat-to-positive print. Invalidates: spot $105 break (pre-print shake-out), or earnings miss + post-print drawdown. Trim discipline ahead of print is the play if position runs further. Position resolves at -50% or expiry.

signals.thesis_setup: Long $1110 call entered 5/1 at $86.40/contract; Sandisk was $1059 at signal (OTM, 4.6% from spot). Entry on cohort-momentum flow: SNDK had been the late-comer to the HBM/NAND-tightness trade, with flow lagging MU by 2-3 weeks. The morning of 5/1 saw the catch-up move: 6 institutional block trades over $400K premium on the May chain plus a repeat-rule fire signaling fresh positioning. Spot rallied to ~$1190 over the week; position is +104% (peak +119%).
signals.thesis_catalyst: Sandisk is the NAND flash leader with the strongest enterprise SSD positioning into AI server buildouts. While HBM (Micron's territory) handles the GPU memory layer, NAND handles the persistent storage layer that AI training datasets require. The structural story is enterprise SSD demand acceleration as AI inference workloads grow. Most recent earnings print was 4/30 (4 days ago); the post-print reaction is the working thesis input. Active near-term catalyst is AMD print Tue AH + cohort readthrough into the broader memory complex.
signals.thesis_macro: Same memory-cohort dynamic as MU: AI-capex second-derivative play, less crowded than the apex names, multiple cushion vs growth-tech average. Tightening tape headwind partially offset by AI demand structural story.
signals.thesis_risk: Stop level $43.20 (50% of entry premium). DTE 11 days. Invalidates: spot $1100 break (gives back the post-print breakout), AMD miss + cohort drawdown that pulls memory cohort down with semis broadly. Less theta-pressured than MU 5/8 because of the longer DTE. Position resolves at -50% or expiry.

signals.thesis_setup: Long XLE $59 call entered 5/1 at $1.43/contract; XLE was around $59 at signal (ATM, 0.1% from spot). Entry on a sector rotation thesis: energy was the only positive sector on 5/1 as oil rallied on geopolitical headlines, and XLE flow showed institutional rotation buying for the first time in three weeks. Position is +25% (peak +34%). Thesis is mid-development: oil $105+ provides the supportive backdrop, but XLE needs continuation higher to extend.
signals.thesis_catalyst: XLE is the energy sector ETF (XOM, CVX, COP, EOG, SLB are top weights). Active catalyst is oil price action ($105/bbl currently, geopolitical risk premium adding to the bid) plus the realized vs expected production discipline from OPEC+. Earnings season for the constituents is mostly past, so individual-name binaries are dilutional. Tertiary catalyst is the broader inflation print rhythm: tight oil + rising yields amplifies the inflation narrative which loops back to XLE positioning.
signals.thesis_macro: Energy is the cleanest tightening-tape-aligned long position: rising yields and dollar firmness signal inflation persistence, which keeps oil prices supported and XLE bid. Position aligns with the macro read in the most direct way possible: sector benefits from the same conditions that pressure growth multiples. AAPL stop today + AMD readthrough are tech-cohort dynamics that don't affect XLE directly.
signals.thesis_risk: Stop level $0.72 (50% of entry premium). DTE 18 days. Invalidates: oil sub-$95/bbl (gives back the geopolitical premium), or sharp dollar weakness that breaks the inflation-narrative coupling. The longer DTE provides cushion through Friday NFP. Position resolves at -50% or expiry.

signals.thesis_setup: Long $200 call entered 5/1 at $8.93/contract; Coinbase was around $205 at signal (ATM, 2.5% ITM). Entry on a Bitcoin price stability + crypto regulatory clarity thesis: BTC had held the $80K area for two weeks, and COIN flow morning of 5/1 showed an institutional buy-block cluster on the May $200-$210 strikes. Spot has rallied modestly; position is +54% (peak +85%). Thesis is mid-flight: needs BTC follow-through to extend.
signals.thesis_catalyst: Coinbase is the largest US-listed crypto exchange with the cleanest regulatory standing post-SEC settlement. Revenue scales directly with retail and institutional crypto trading volume; spot ETF flows (especially IBIT) drive incremental volume to Coinbase as the prime brokerage. Active catalyst is BTC price action plus any regulatory clarity from the new administration's framework. Earnings 5/8 (next week, inside DTE) is a binary catalyst with IV crush risk.
signals.thesis_macro: Crypto carries multi-factor sensitivity: BTC price as primary driver, regulatory tape as secondary, overall risk-on vs risk-off positioning as tertiary. Tightening tape is generally a headwind for crypto multiples but BTC has proven decoupled from yields recently. Position aligns with BTC as long as $80K holds.
signals.thesis_risk: Stop level $4.47 (50% of entry premium). DTE 11 days. Earnings 5/8 inside DTE: post-print IV typically drops by ~50%, eating into option premium even on a flat print. Invalidates: BTC sub-$75K, COIN earnings miss with reduced volume guidance, or any regulatory surprise. Trim ahead of print is the discipline. Position resolves at -50% or expiry.

signals.thesis_setup: Long $400 call entered 5/1 at $10.82/contract; Tesla was around $385 at signal (OTM, 3.9% from spot). Entry on a robotaxi + Optimus narrative repositioning: TSLA had been beaten down through April on demand concerns, and the flow morning of 5/1 showed institutional positioning around the upcoming robotaxi service launch in Austin. Spot has chopped between $360-$385 since entry; position is currently -20% (peak +12%). Thesis is mid-development; underlying needs to break $400 to validate.
signals.thesis_catalyst: Tesla's near-term catalyst is the robotaxi service expansion (Austin operational, Phoenix coming) plus FSD v13 software rollout improving consumer-side adoption. Optimus humanoid robot is a longer-arc story but each demo gets re-rated. EV demand normalization is the headwind: deliveries have been flat-to-down, margin pressure persistent. Earnings are past (4/22); next print 7/23 outside DTE. Position resolves on tape + narrative, not earnings.
signals.thesis_macro: Tesla carries sentiment-beta that exceeds its fundamentals: the stock moves on Musk-narrative cycles more than EV demand. Tightening tape is mixed: growth multiple compression is a headwind, but robotaxi narrative is sentiment-positive when it surfaces. The position is fighting both sector rotation and stock-specific demand concerns.
signals.thesis_risk: Stop level $5.41 (50% of entry premium). DTE 11 days. Invalidates: spot $370 break (gives back the post-earnings hold), or any robotaxi rollout setback news. The position needs an explicit catalyst to recover; flat tape continuation is theta-eroding. Position resolves at -50% or expiry.

signals.thesis_setup: Long $150 call entered 5/1 at $18.93/contract; Nebius was $141 at signal (OTM, 5.8% from spot). Entry on a fresh flow burst around the European AI cloud narrative: NBIS is the Yandex-spinoff that exclusively serves EU/Israel AI compute markets with NVDA GPU allocation. Flow that morning showed two institutional block trades over $300K premium on the May chain plus a 2.4x unusual-volume read. Spot has rallied to $158 area; position is +81% (peak +89%). The 5/29 DTE provides the longest cushion of any open position; earnings 5/13 fits well inside.
signals.thesis_catalyst: Nebius operates AI cloud datacenters in Finland and Israel with anchor NVDA GPU allocation; recent disclosure of expanded Microsoft + Oracle hyperscaler discussions broadens customer TAM beyond the original niche. Most recent earnings 4/30 confirmed cluster expansion; the next print 5/13 sits inside DTE and is a binary catalyst with IV crush risk. Active catalyst beyond earnings is any new hyperscaler partnership disclosure or NVDA Blackwell allocation expansion. The 5/13 print is the dominant near-term move.
signals.thesis_macro: AI cloud pure-plays carry beta to risk-off broadly, but NBIS has the cleanest sovereign-AI angle for European institutional investors who can't or won't buy CRWV. Tightening tape is a headwind, but the geographic + GPU-allocation moat differentiates. AMD print Tue AH provides cohort readthrough.
signals.thesis_risk: Stop level $9.46 (50% of entry premium). DTE 25 days, theta is moderate. Earnings 5/13 inside DTE creates IV crush risk: implied vol is around 85% on the 5/13 weekly chain, post-print should drop to ~45%. Invalidates: spot $135 break (pre-print failure), or 5/13 earnings miss with capacity guidance reset. Position resolves at -50% or expiry.

signals.thesis_setup: Long AAPL $275 call entered 5/1 at $11.66/contract; Apple was around $213 at signal (deep OTM, 29% from spot). Entry on a power-grid + AI capex theme: AAPL had been a laggard on the AI capex narrative as institutional positioning crowded into MSFT/GOOGL/MU. Thesis was that an AI on-device acceleration commentary at the upcoming WWDC keynote would re-rate AAPL's AI exposure. Spot rallied to $214 then chopped between $210-$215 for two days; position hit the published rule -50% on 5/4 as AI-energy/power-grid theme broke under tightening pressure. Loss locked at -53.9%.
signals.thesis_catalyst: Apple's AI exposure is on-device (Apple Silicon NPUs, Neural Engine) plus the rumored expanded Apple Intelligence rollout. Thesis required a catalyst-driven re-rate of AAPL's positioning relative to AI-capex peers. WWDC was outside DTE (June), so the position depended on pre-announcement leak/positioning. The flow was correct directionally for two sessions, then the macro tape rolled.
signals.thesis_macro: AAPL was the cohort name without a near-term earnings catalyst (4/30 print past). When the macro shifted hard to tightening on 5/4 (yields broke 4.42%, dollar firmed), AI-capex names without an immediate event got sold first. Signal lesson: in regime-shift environments, names that lack an idiosyncratic upcoming catalyst are sold first; this validates the discipline of weighting near-term-catalyst names higher in the methodology.
signals.thesis_risk: Stop hit at -50% on 5/4 around 11:00 ET. Final close at -53.9% as spot continued to drift toward $213.50 area. Held-to-expiry view: thesis not yet resolved (calendar expiry 5/15). Strict-stop view: closed at the published rule, full loss locked. The position resolved cleanly within the published framework.

signals.thesis_setup: Long $22 call entered 5/1 at $1.72/contract; TeraWulf was around $19 at signal (OTM, 13.5% from spot). Entry on a specific flow signature: WULF is a small-cap Bitcoin miner pivoting toward AI HPC hosting, and the May $22 strike caught two institutional block trades over $80K premium - small in absolute terms but unusual relative to WULF's typical option volume. Thesis was AI hosting pivot validation. Spot has chopped between $19-$21; position is currently +6% (peak +20%). Position needs underlying continuation to extend.
signals.thesis_catalyst: TeraWulf operates Bitcoin mining facilities in upstate New York and Pennsylvania with available power capacity being reconfigured for AI HPC hosting customers. Active catalyst is any AI hosting customer contract disclosure: WULF announced a major tenant earlier this year and the question is contract extension and additional buildouts. Bitcoin price stability ($80K area) supports the legacy mining cash flow that funds the AI capex pivot. No earnings in DTE.
signals.thesis_macro: Small-cap status + crypto correlation = double-headed beta. Tightening tape that pressures small caps and crypto simultaneously hits hard. The offset is the AI-hosting structural story which provides idiosyncratic upside if a contract is announced.
signals.thesis_risk: Stop level $0.86 (50% of entry premium). DTE 11 days. Invalidates: BTC sub-$75K (legacy mining cash flow risk), or WULF spot $18 break (pre-contract-announcement failure). The 5/8 expiry on related WULF chains expires this week which could remove a flow tailwind. Position resolves at -50% or expiry.

signals.thesis_setup: Long $440 call entered 5/1 at $6.80/contract; Broadcom was $432 at signal (OTM, 1.9% from spot). Entry on an AI-networking + custom-silicon thesis: AVGO had been the institutional 'durable AI compounder' play, with steady flow that lacked the burst signature of the MU/CRWV trades. Spot has chopped between $415-$435 since entry; position is currently -35% (peak +7%). The position is underwater because the underlying has not provided directional follow-through, and theta has eroded the OTM premium without IV expansion to offset.
signals.thesis_catalyst: Broadcom's AI exposure is two-pronged: networking ASIC business (Tomahawk + Jericho switches that move data between AI accelerators in datacenters) and custom-silicon design wins (Google TPUs, Meta MTIA chips, hyperscaler-bespoke compute). Plus the legacy VMware integration that improves software-side margin. Earnings 6/3 (outside DTE), so this resolves on flow continuation + AI capex narrative, not an event binary. The thesis hasn't broken; it's just stalled while the apex names extended.
